keyMyths.title: The Establishment of Babylonian Dominance
keyMyths.description: Hammurabi, inheriting a relatively small kingdom, skillfully employed diplomacy and military might to unite Mesopotamia under Babylonian rule. This involved conquering rival city-states like Larsa and Mari, establishing Babylon as the central power.

keyMyths.title: The Legal Code: Divine Mandate and Social Order
keyMyths.description: Hammurabi claimed his law code was divinely inspired by Shamash, the god of justice, imbuing it with religious authority. The code aimed to establish social order and protect the weak, though it also codified existing social hierarchies.

keyMyths.title: Infrastructure Development and Economic Prosperity
keyMyths.description: Hammurabi invested heavily in infrastructure projects, including canals for irrigation and flood control. These projects boosted agricultural production and facilitated trade, leading to economic prosperity throughout Babylonia.

keyMyths.title: Military Campaigns Against Elam and Eshnunna
keyMyths.description: Hammurabi faced military challenges from neighboring kingdoms like Elam and Eshnunna. His successful campaigns against these rivals secured Babylonia's borders and solidified its position as a regional power.

keyMyths.title: The Restoration of Ancient Temples and Cults
keyMyths.description: Hammurabi undertook the restoration of numerous temples dedicated to various deities throughout Babylonia. This act demonstrated his piety and strengthened his legitimacy as a ruler chosen by the gods, reinforcing the state religion.

achievements: Promoted economic prosperity through trade and agriculture
Secured Babylonian borders through military campaigns
Elevated the status of Babylon as a major cultural and political center

sources.name: The Code of Hammurabi
sources.description: A comprehensive collection of laws inscribed on a stele, providing insights into Babylonian society and legal principles.
sources.title: The Code of Hammurabi

sources.name: Royal Inscriptions of Hammurabi
sources.description: Inscriptions detailing Hammurabi's military campaigns, building projects, and religious dedications.
sources.title: Royal Inscriptions of Hammurabi

sources.name: Administrative Texts from Hammurabi's Reign
sources.description: Clay tablets documenting economic activity, land management, and bureaucratic procedures.
sources.title: Administrative Texts from Hammurabi's Reign

sources.name: Victory Stelae and Chronicles
sources.description: Monuments commemorating Hammurabi's military victories and providing historical narratives of his reign.
sources.title: Victory Stelae and Chronicles

sources.name: Temple Inscriptions and Building Records
sources.description: Inscriptions and records documenting the restoration and construction of temples during Hammurabi's rule.
sources.title: Temple Inscriptions and Building Records
